name: Caramel Layer Choco-Squares
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Reserve 2 tablespoons of evaporated milk for the cake mixture.
3. In a medium saucepan over low heat, combine the remaining evaporated milk and caramels. Stir constantly until the caramels are melted. Set aside.
4. In a large bowl, mix the cake mix, nuts, margarine, and reserved 2 tablespoons of evaporated milk on low speed until crumbly.
5. Press half of the dough mixture into the bottom of an ungreased 13x9-inch pan. Reserve the remaining dough mixture for topping.
6. Bake for 8 minutes.
7. Sprinkle chocolate chips evenly over the partially baked crust.
8. Carefully spread the caramel mixture over the chocolate chips.
9. Crumble the reserved dough mixture over the caramel mixture.
10. Return to oven and bake an additional 15-18 minutes, or until the filling is set. Cool completely.
11. Cut into bars.
Source: Pillsbury Sweet Sensations Cookbook
